errors.apiUnreachable: We can't reach the server right now. Check your connection, make sure the service is running, and try again.
errors.unexpected: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.validation: Some fields need fixing. Check the form and try again.
errors.badRequest: That action isn't valid. Please check your data and try again.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.notFound: We couldn't find what you're looking for.
errors.conflict: That conflicts with existing data. Refresh the page or try a different value.
errors.server: The server is busy. Please try again in a moment.
errors.session: Your session has ended. Please sign in again.
errors.formLoadFailed: We couldn't load this form. Check your connection and try again.
errors.pdfDownloadFailed: The PDF couldn't be downloaded. Please try again.

hero.title: Lot traceability you can verify
hero.lead: SupplyTrack helps producers share the story of each production lot—from farm or pond through processing to the shelf. As a member of the public, you can confirm what you’re buying by scanning a code on the package. No account is required.

diagram.consumerLead: Three simple steps—no app and no login.
diagram.consumerAria: Consumer flow: packaging, scan, trace page
diagram.c1Body: Each production lot has a unique code on the label or box.
diagram.c2Body: The camera reads the link and opens this site in your browser.
diagram.c3Body: See specifications, partners, and the published timeline for that batch.
diagram.chainLead: Operators record each checkpoint; you only see what they publish for that lot.
diagram.chainSvgAria: Diagram: harvest and pond, processing, label with QR, consumer scan

lookup.title: See information for a lot (public)
lookup.subtitle: Scan the QR on the package—or enter the lot code
lookup.scanBody: Open your camera app and point it at the QR printed on the product. Tap the link that appears—you’ll get the full trace page for that lot. No app install and no login.
lookup.hint: The same code is embedded in the QR. You can copy it from the label, a receipt, or an email from the supplier.
lookup.label: Lot code

about.title: About this application
about.p1: This website hosts two experiences. The public side (what you are seeing now) explains how traceability works and is the entry point when someone scans a QR code printed on product packaging. Each code opens a dedicated page for that lot, with information supplied by the producer and their supply-chain partners.
about.p2: Behind the scenes, authorized staff use a separate, password-protected area to register products, actors (farms, labs, warehouses, etc.), production lots, and traceability events. That data is what powers the public lot page you see after a scan.

public.title: What you can do here as the public
public.intro: Everything below is available without signing in, as long as you have a valid link or QR code from the product.
public.f1: Scan a QR code on the label or box with your phone camera (or open the link the producer gave you). You will land on a trace page for that specific lot.
public.f2: See product and lot details—for example name, SKU, presentation, packaging, size grade, certifications, lot code, and key quality attributes the producer chose to publish.
public.f3: Review harvest and origin information, such as harvest date, pool or pond, and which farm, lab, maturation, and co-packing partners were involved for that batch.
public.f4: Follow the traceability timeline—a chronological list of events (e.g. harvested, transported, quality checked, stored, shipped) with who performed each step and when.
public.f5: Download the lot QR image or use print-friendly options on the trace page when you need a paper copy for records or display.
public.f6: Browse read-only. You cannot change data, create lots, or see other consumers’ activity—only the information published for that lot.
public.note: Trace URLs on this site follow the pattern /trace/<lot-code>—the same address embedded in the QR on packaging.

steps.title: How to view a lot in three steps
steps.s1: Get a code — Find the QR on the product or use a trace link from the retailer or producer.
steps.s2: Scan or tap — Your browser opens this site on the lot’s public trace page.
steps.s3: Explore — Scroll through specifications, participants, and the event history for that batch.

cards.publicBody: If you only want to verify a product you purchased, use the QR or link—you do not need an operator account or anything on this landing page except the explanation above.
cards.opsBody: Signed-in users with the right role maintain the catalogue, lots, events, and (where allowed) user accounts. This is not where consumers go after scanning a package.
cards.opsFineprint: Your organization may restrict registration; use credentials from your administrator if self-sign-up is turned off.

landing.footer: SupplyTrack · Transparent lot-level traceability for consumers and operators
